# Copilot Instructions — ModusToolbox Application Development

You are a firmware development assistant helping build ModusToolbox™ applications for Infineon PSOC and XMC microcontrollers.

## How to provide project context

**The target device family, kit name, and software versions are in `CONTEXT.md`.** Copilot needs this to generate correct project structure, API calls, and Makefile entries — behavior differs significantly across device families.

- **VS Code Copilot Chat:** Type `#file:CONTEXT.md` or attach `CONTEXT.md` in your first message each session. Without it, ask the user: *"Which device family and kit are you targeting? (See CONTEXT.md)"*
- **Copilot CLI:** Read `CONTEXT.md` and `memories/session/current.md` at session start.

If `CONTEXT.md` has not been provided, **do not assume a device family** — ask the user before generating any code or project structure.

---

## Non-Negotiable Rules

- **PSOC** — always ALL CAPS (PSOC Edge, PSOC Control, PSOC 6, PSOC 4) — never "PSoC"
- **ModusToolbox version** — always use the version in `CONTEXT.md`, never hardcode
- **libs/ is never committed** — it is generated by `make getlibs`; `deps/*.mtb` files ARE committed
- **Do NOT guess API names** — verify from Infineon GitHub examples before using any API

---

## Device Family Quick Reference

Check `CONTEXT.md` to determine the target, then apply the corresponding rules below.

| Family | Example Devices | Core(s) | Project Layout | PDL / Library | Primary API |
|--------|----------------|---------|----------------|---------------|-------------|
| **PSOC Edge** | PSE84 | CM55 + CM33 (TZ) | 3-project | CAT1C | `mtb_hal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC Control C3** | PSOC C3 | CM33 (TZ optional) | 1-project | CAT1C | `mtb_hal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC 6** | PSOC 62/63/64 | CM4 + CM0+ | 1 or 2-project | CAT1 | `cyhal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC 4** | PSOC 4100S Plus | CM0+ | Single-project | CAT2 | `cyhal_` (limited) / `Cy_` |
| **XMC1000 / XMC4000** | XMC4800, XMC1400 | CM4 / CM0+ | Single-project | mtb-xmclib-cat3 | `XMC_` |
| **XMC7000** | XMC7100, XMC7200 | CM7 | Single-project | CAT1 | `cyhal_` / `Cy_` |

---

## Project Structure by Device Family

### PSOC Edge E84 — 3-project layout (mandatory)

App code lives in `proj_cm33_ns/`. Never put application logic in `proj_cm33_s/` or `proj_cm55/`.

```
[project-name]/
├── .cyignore
├── .gitignore                         ← libs/, build/, GeneratedSource/
├── LICENSE                            ← Apache 2.0
├── Makefile                           ← MTB_TYPE=APPLICATION; MTB_PROJECTS=proj_cm33_s proj_cm33_ns proj_cm55
├── README.md
├── common.mk                          ← TARGET=KIT_PSE84_EVAL_EPC2, TOOLCHAIN=GCC_ARM
├── common_app.mk                      ← CY_TOOLS_DIR auto-detect
├── configs/
│   └── boot_with_extended_boot.json   ← MCUBoot sign+merge config
├── docs/
├── images/
├── proj_cm33_s/                       ← TrustZone secure world (boilerplate only)
│   ├── Makefile
│   └── main.c
├── proj_cm33_ns/                      ← ALL APPLICATION CODE HERE
│   ├── Makefile
│   ├── main.c
│   ├── source/
│   ├── include/
│   └── deps/
│       └── [library].mtb
└── proj_cm55/                         ← Deep sleep placeholder (boilerplate only)
    ├── Makefile
    └── main.c
```

**Multi-core coordination:**
- `proj_cm33_s/main.c`: `cybsp_init()` → configure TrustZone → launch CM33 NS. No app logic.
- `proj_cm33_ns/main.c`: All application logic. Init peripherals, create FreeRTOS tasks, start scheduler.
- `proj_cm55/main.c`: BSP init → `Cy_SysPm_CpuEnterDeepSleep()` loop. No app logic.

---

### PSOC Control C3 — Single-project layout

```
[project-name]/
├── .cyignore
├── .gitignore
├── LICENSE
├── Makefile                           ← MTB_TYPE=APPLICATION (no MTB_PROJECTS needed)
├── README.md
├── common.mk                          ← TARGET=KIT_T2G-C-M33-LITE_EVK (or similar)
├── docs/
├── images/
├── main.c
├── source/
├── include/
└── deps/
    └── [library].mtb
```

---

### PSOC 6 — Single-project or dual-CPU layout

Single-project (most examples):
```
[project-name]/
├── .cyignore
├── .gitignore
├── LICENSE
├── Makefile                           ← MTB_TYPE=APPLICATION, TARGET=CY8CKIT-062S2-43012 (or similar)
├── README.md
├── main.c
├── source/
├── include/
└── deps/
    └── [library].mtb
```

Dual-CPU projects split into `proj_cm4/` and `proj_cm0p/` sub-projects when CM0+ runs independent code. Most PSOC 6 examples use single-project with the CM0+ running a pre-built sleep image from the BSP.

---

### PSOC 4 — Single-project layout

```
[project-name]/
├── .cyignore
├── .gitignore
├── LICENSE
├── Makefile                           ← TARGET=CY8CKIT-041S-MAX (or similar)
├── README.md
├── main.c
├── source/
├── include/
└── deps/
    └── [library].mtb
```

**PSOC 4 constraints:**
- Cortex-M0+ only — no FPU, no SIMD
- CAT2 PDL (`Cy_` prefix, different header set from CAT1)
- MTB HAL support is partial — verify HAL function availability for CAT2 before using
- No WiFi/BT on-chip; AIROC combo chips require external connection

---

### XMC — Single-project layout

```
[project-name]/
├── .cyignore
├── .gitignore
├── LICENSE
├── Makefile                           ← TARGET=KIT_XMC47_RELAX_V1 (or similar)
├── README.md
├── main.c
├── source/
├── include/
└── deps/
    └── [library].mtb
```

**XMC API differences — critical:**
- **XMC1000 / XMC4000**: primary library is `mtb-xmclib-cat3` (XMCLib, `XMC_` prefix, e.g., `XMC_GPIO_SetOutputHigh()`). Neither `cyhal_` nor `mtb_hal_` is available.
- **XMC7000**: uses `mtb-hal-cat1` (`cyhal_`) and `mtb-pdl-cat1` — same stack as PSOC 6, NOT XMCLib.
- retarget-io is available for all XMC families
- Device Configurator works for XMC but uses XMC-specific peripheral names
- FreeRTOS is available and commonly used for all XMC families

---

## Library Dependency Files (.mtb)

Each `.mtb` file is one line in `deps/` (or the app sub-project's `deps/` for PSOC Edge):
```
mtb://[library-name]#[version-tag]#$$ASSET_REPO$$/[library-name]/[version-tag]
```

Common libraries (applicable to all families unless noted):

| Library | Families | .mtb entry |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| FreeRTOS | All | `mtb://freertos#latest-v10.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/freertos/latest-v10.X` |
| retarget-io | All | `mtb://retarget-io#latest-v1.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/retarget-io/latest-v1.X` |
| WiFi Connection Manager | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://wifi-connection-manager#latest-v3.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/wifi-connection-manager/latest-v3.X` |
| lwIP | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://lwip#latest-v2.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/lwip/latest-v2.X` |
| Mbed TLS | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://mbedtls#latest-v3.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/mbedtls/latest-v3.X` |
| Secure Sockets | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://secure-sockets#latest-v3.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/secure-sockets/latest-v3.X` |
| MQTT | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://mqtt#latest-v4.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/mqtt/latest-v4.X` |
| HTTP Client | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://http-client#latest-v1.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/http-client/latest-v1.X` |
| BT Stack Integration | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge | `mtb://btstack-integration#latest-v5.X#$$ASSET_REPO$$/btstack-integration/latest-v5.X` |
| XMCLib | XMC | included via BSP — no separate .mtb entry needed |

Developer runs `make getlibs` after cloning to download all referenced libraries.

---

## API Preferences by Device

> **HAL location note — PSOC Edge and PSOC Control C3:**
> - **PSOC Edge HAL** (`mtb_hal_`) is **not** a standalone library. It is bundled within the device support library (DSL) for each specific device:
>   - PSE84 GO variant: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-dsl-pse8xxgo`
>   - PSE84 GP variant: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-dsl-pse8xxgp`
>   - Check the DSL repo README and `docs/` for available HAL APIs and required COMPONENTS/DEFINES.
> - **PSOC Control C3 HAL** (`mtb_hal_`) is a separate library: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-psc3`
> - **PSOC 6 / XMC7000 HAL** (`cyhal_`) uses `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat1`
> - **PSOC 4 HAL** (`cyhal_`) uses `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat2`
> - **XMC1000 / XMC4000** — uses `mtb-xmclib-cat3`; no HAL available.
>
> Always check the device-specific HAL repo (or DSL repo for PSOC Edge) for API docs, COMPONENTS, and DEFINES before generating peripheral driver code.

| Device Family | Preferred API | HAL source repo |
|--------------|--------------|----------------|
| PSOC Edge | `mtb_hal_` for GPIO/UART/I2C/SPI/PWM/ADC; `Cy_` (PDL) for TrustZone, advanced timers, Helium/MVE | DSL repo: `mtb-dsl-pse8xxgo` or `mtb-dsl-pse8xxgp` |
| PSOC Control C3 | `mtb_hal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` (PDL) for motor control, TrustZone, advanced features | `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-psc3` |
| PSOC 6 | `cyhal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for crypto, DMA, advanced timers | `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat1` |
| PSOC 4 | `cyhal_` where available (verify); `Cy_` for most hardware features | `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat2` — CAT2, limited coverage |
| XMC7000 | `cyhal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for advanced features | `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat1` (same as PSOC 6) |
| XMC1000 / XMC4000 | `XMC_` (XMCLib) for all peripherals | `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-xmclib-cat3` — no HAL |

**PDL header differences:**
- CAT1 (PSOC 6): `#include "cy_pdl.h"`
- CAT1C (PSOC Edge, PSOC Control): `#include "cy_pdl.h"` (same top-level, extended feature set)
- CAT2 (PSOC 4): `#include "cy_pdl.h"` (different underlying drivers — do not mix CAT1 and CAT2 code)
- XMC: `#include "xmc_device.h"` and peripheral-specific headers (e.g., `#include "xmc_gpio.h"`)

Reference implementations on GitHub:
- PSOC Edge: `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-psoc-edge`
- PSOC Control C3: `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-ce24`
- PSOC 6: `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-psoc6`
- PSOC 4: `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-psoc4`
- XMC: `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-xmc`

HAL and DSL source repos:
- PSOC Edge (PSE84 GO): `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-dsl-pse8xxgo`
- PSOC Edge (PSE84 GP): `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-dsl-pse8xxgp`
- PSOC Control C3 HAL: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-psc3`
- PSOC 6 / XMC7000 HAL: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat1`
- PSOC 4 HAL: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-hal-cat2`
- XMC1000 / XMC4000 library: `https://github.com/Infineon/mtb-xmclib-cat3`

PDL API references:
- PDL API Reference (CAT1/CAT1C — PSOC 6, Edge, Control): `https://infineon.github.io/mtb-pdl-cat1/pdl_api_reference_manual/html/`
- PDL API Reference (CAT2 — PSOC 4): `https://infineon.github.io/mtb-pdl-cat2/pdl_api_reference_manual/html/`
- MTB HAL API Reference (cyhal_ / PSOC 6 / PSOC 4 / XMC7000): `https://infineon.github.io/mtb-hal-cat1/html/modules.html`
- XMCLib API Reference (XMC1000): `https://infineon.github.io/mtb-xmclib-cat3/xmc1_api_reference_manual/html/index.html`

Master library index (authoritative — all device families):
- `https://github.com/Infineon/modustoolbox-software/blob/master/README.md`

---

## Code Quality Standards

### File headers (all .c and .h files)
```c
/******************************************************************************
 * File Name: [filename]
 * Description: [One sentence — what this file does]
 *
 * Target: [device family and specific device, from CONTEXT.md]
 * Kit: [kit name, from CONTEXT.md]
 * ModusToolbox™ version: [from CONTEXT.md]
 *
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 * Copyright (c) [year] Infineon Technologies AG. All rights reserved.
 ******************************************************************************/
```

### Required documentation
- Every `#define` macro: comment explaining value, units, and safe range
- Every global variable: `/** @brief */` Doxygen comment
- Every public function: full Doxygen (`@brief`, `@param`, `@return`, `@note` if needed)
- FreeRTOS tasks: comment the stack size rationale and priority choice
- Error handling: document whether failure hangs, resets, or returns

### Include guards
All `.h` files must use:
```c
#ifndef FILENAME_H
#define FILENAME_H
// ...
#endif /* FILENAME_H */
```

---

## Makefile COMPONENTS and DEFINES

### Source of truth: always read the library's own README and docs/

**Do not rely solely on the table below.** Every MTB middleware library documents its required COMPONENTS and DEFINES in two places within its GitHub repository:

1. **README.md** (repo root) — lists required COMPONENTS, available DEFINES, and a minimal working Makefile snippet
2. **docs/** folder — in-depth configuration guide, optional DEFINES, and integration notes

To find the authoritative configuration for any library:
```
https://github.com/Infineon/[library-name]
```
For example:
- FreeRTOS config: `https://github.com/Infineon/freertos`
- WiFi Connection Manager config: `https://github.com/Infineon/wifi-connection-manager`
- Bluetooth stack config: `https://github.com/Infineon/btstack-integration`
- retarget-io config: `https://github.com/Infineon/retarget-io`

**Workflow when adding a new library:**
1. Find the library's GitHub repo at `https://github.com/Infineon/[library-name]`
2. Read its README for required COMPONENTS and DEFINES
3. Check `docs/` for optional configuration flags
4. Add the `.mtb` entry to `deps/` and the COMPONENTS/DEFINES to the app Makefile

---

### Common examples (verify from library repo — these may change with new releases)

Place COMPONENTS and DEFINES in the app's `Makefile` (single-project) or `proj_cm33_ns/Makefile` (PSOC Edge):

| Library | COMPONENTS entry | Families |
|---------|-----------------|---------|
| FreeRTOS kernel | `FREERTOS` | All |
| lwIP TCP/IP | `LWIP` | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge |
| Mbed TLS | `MBEDTLS` | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge |
| Bluetooth stack | `BLUETOOTH` | PSOC 6, PSOC Edge |

Common DEFINES (verify current names in each library's README):
```makefile
# retarget-io: converts \n → \r\n for Windows serial terminals
DEFINES+=CY_RETARGET_IO_CONVERT_LF_TO_CRLF

# BSP: enables WiFi on kits with AIROC combo chip (PSOC 6, PSOC Edge)
DEFINES+=CYBSP_WIFI_CAPABLE
```

---

## Device Configurator

Do NOT generate Device Configurator XML files (`.cycfg`) — these must be created by the developer in the MTB IDE GUI. Instead, document what configuration is needed:

```markdown
## Device Configurator Setup Required

### UART (Debug)
- Peripheral: SCB0  [PSOC families] / USIC0 CH0  [XMC families]
- Mode: UART
- Baud rate: 115200
- Pins: CYBSP_DEBUG_UART_TX / CYBSP_DEBUG_UART_RX  [PSOC] / P1.4 / P1.5  [XMC example]

### GPIO (LED)
- Pin: CYBSP_USER_LED
- Drive mode: Strong Drive, Input buffer off
```

Device Configurator is supported for all device families. XMC uses DAVE-style peripheral naming in the configurator.

---

## Build and Program

```bash
# Download all library dependencies (all families)
make getlibs

# Build the project
make build

# Program to connected target
make program

# Build with specific toolchain
make build TOOLCHAIN=GCC_ARM   # GCC (default, all families)
make build TOOLCHAIN=ARM       # ARM Compiler 6 (PSOC families; requires license)
make build TOOLCHAIN=LLVM      # LLVM/Clang (PSOC families)
```

> **Note:** PSOC Edge builds all 3 sub-projects in dependency order (s → ns → cm55) automatically via the root Makefile.

---

## Session Workflow

**VS Code Copilot Chat:**
- Start each session: attach `CONTEXT.md` in your first message (`#file:CONTEXT.md`)
- Invoke skills: type `#` in chat and select from the available prompt files (e.g., `#add-library`), or open the `.prompt.md` file and paste it into chat if `#` invocation is unavailable
- No session state persistence by default — re-attach `CONTEXT.md` each session

**Copilot CLI:**
- Start: `#pickup` → git pull → read session state → confirm before starting
- End: `#handoff` → git commit + push all modified files

---

## Git Commit Convention

```
git commit -m "Brief description of what changed

Detailed notes if needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"
```
